clauses.clause:
5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Inventions and Discoveries
The Employee acknowledges and agrees that any and all technical innovations, designs, trade secrets, software code, and discoveries made, conceived, or reduced to practice by the Employee during official hours of employment are the sole and exclusive property of the Company.

subclauses.subclause text: The Employee acknowledges and agrees that any and all technical innovations, designs, trade secrets, software code, and discoveries made, conceived, or reduced to practice by the Employee during official hours of employment are the sole and exclusive property of the Company.

clauses.clause:
2. OWNERSHIP FRAMEWORKS
2.3 Employee-Developed Software Adjustments
Any application software tool, proprietary script, or creative work developed by an employee utilizing any company hardware, network infrastructure, or material resources—regardless of whether such activities occur during or outside of official working hours—remains the exclusive personal property of the individual employee unless assigned explicitly via a standalone written assignment instrument.

subclauses.subclause text: Any application software tool, proprietary script, or creative work developed by an employee utilizing any company hardware, network infrastructure, or material resources—regardless of whether such activities occur during or outside of official working hours—remains the exclusive personal property of the individual employee unless assigned explicitly via a standalone written assignment instrument.

clauses.clause:
7. COVENANTS AGAINST COMPETITION
7.2 Territorial Boundaries
The Employee shall not, for a period of twelve months post-separation, directly or indirectly engage with or provide professional services to any competing corporate entity operating within a strict fifty-mile radius of the Company’s primary headquarters.

subclauses.subclause text: The Employee shall not, for a period of twelve months post-separation, directly or indirectly engage with or provide professional services to any competing corporate entity operating within a strict fifty-mile radius of the Company’s primary headquarters.

clauses.clause:
6. GLOBAL COMPETITIVE RESTRICTIONS
6.1 Universal Non-Compete Application
To preserve market stability and proprietary trade secrets, the restrictive non-compete parameters binding all affiliated staff members apply globally, spanning all international geographic markets and municipal territories where the Company currently distributes or markets its technical solutions.

subclauses.subclause text: To preserve market stability and proprietary trade secrets, the restrictive non-compete parameters binding all affiliated staff members apply globally, spanning all international geographic markets and municipal territories where the Company currently distributes or markets its technical solutions.
